messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
Who is the traditionally attributed author of the Meditations?

The Meditations (沉思錄) is attributed to Marcus Aurelius, the Roman emperor and Stoic philosopher who lived from 121 to 180 CE. The work is a collection of personal philosophical reflections he wrote in Greek, apparently as a private journal rather than a text intended for publication. The attribution to Marcus Aurelius is well-supported and considered reliable. It should not be attributed to Plato, Socrates, or Epictetus, though Marcus Aurelius was strongly influenced by Stoic teachers including Epictetus.
